text:
Paris 2024 Olympics Highlights:Dhiraj Bommadevara finished 4th, Tarundeep Rai 14th and Pravin Jadhav 39th in the men’s archery ranking round as India finished 3rd in the team rankings.

Earlier, India made a direct qualification in the women’s team archery by finishing fourth in the ranking round. Ankita Bhakat finished 11th with 666 points (her season best), Bhajan Kaur was 22nd with 659 points while Deepika Kumari 23rd with 658 points.

The Paris Olympics Opening Ceremony will be held on July 26 from 11 PM IST onwards.

Catch all the latest updates from the Paris Olympics events being held on Thursday, July 25, 2024. Stay updated as India’s Paris 2024 Olympics campaign begins today with archery ranking round in Les Invalides.

India’s campaign at the 2024 Paris Olympics got underway today with a host of archers in action in the women’s and men’s individual ranking rounds. Ankita Bhakat, Bhajan Kaur and Deepika Kumari have secured 11th, 22nd and 23rd seedings respectively for the individual event while also qualifying for the women’s team quarters.

India have never won an Olympic archery medal.

The qualification round decides the seedings for the knockout competition slated to get underway from Sunday. The 128 archers (64 female + 64 male) have to shoot 72 arrows each which determines their rankings for the single-elimination bracket.

The top-ranked archer takes on the lowest-ranked archer, the second-ranked against the second-lowest and so on.

As for the team events, the same rankings are used to determine seedings.

For men’s/women’s team events, the total of the archer’s scores from is used while for mixed team event, the total of men’s and women’s from each nation is used.

The top four seeded teams in the men’s and women’s events are given a direct entry into the quarters while those seeded between 5 and 12 will square off in round of 16 with the winner advancing into the quarters.

In the mixed team event though only the top-16 get an entry.

Stay updated with the latest fromParis Olympics 2024. ExploreParis Olympics 2024 events today. Check the updated list ofmedal tally for Paris Olympics 2024. CheckParis Olympics 2024 event results.

Stay updated on theParis 2024 Olympics Opening Ceremony with our live blog here.

The Indian men’s team will take on either Turkiye or Colombia in the quarter-finals and may run into South Korea in the semi-finals.

Dhiraj Bommadevara along with Ankita Bhakat have qualified for round of 16 in the mixed archery team event. Bhakat had scored 666 points to finish 11th as Dhiraj scored 681 points to finish 4th.

India finish 3rd after the ranking round with a total of 2013 points. South Korea tops the list with 2049, followed by France with 2025 as China is fourth to round off the quarterfinalists.

India are in 6th spot at the halway stage, tied with China on 1000 points.



India up into the 5th spot.



India is currently ranked 6th.



Tarundeep Rai (11B), Pravin Ramesh Jadhav (10B) and Dhiraj Bommadevara (12B) are representing India in the men’s individual qualifications.

Age –40

India’s Tarundeep Rai, Pravin Ramesh Jadhav and Dhiraj Bommadevara will be taking part in the Paris Olympics’ men’s individual recurve qualifications.

India started its Paris Olympics campaign on a bright note a day ahead of the Opening Ceremony with the women’s team securing a fourth-place finish in the ranking round to seal a spot in the quarterfinal stage at Les Invalides here on Thursday. In the quarterfinals, India will face the winner of the last-16 stage match between hosts France and the Netherlands. The team finished with a combined score of 1983.

In the individual round, Ankita Bhakat bagged the 11th spot with a season-best score of 666 after 72 shots, including 30 tens and 9 Xs. She picked 54 points in the final end. Bhajan Kaur scored 659 (25 10s and 6 Xs)to finish 22nd in the standings. Deepika Kumari, who is competing in her fourth Olympics, settled for 23rd place in the final standings with 658 points (28 10s and 6 Xs).

Here are the opponents India women archers will face in the Round of 32 Elimination

India have finished fourth in the team’s event having amassed a total of 1983 points and have thus secured a direct entry into the quarter-finals. Republic of Korea topped this ranking round setting a new Olympic record of 2046 points while China (1996) and Mexico (1986) finished 2nd and 3rd respectively.

The women’s individual ranking round is over and Ankita Bhakat is the best-placed Indian with 666 points to claim 11th spot. Bhajan Kaur has accumulated 659 points to claim the 22nd spot while Deepika Kumari is 23rd with 658 points.

It will be a Herculean task to match the Tokyo figures of seven medals, given that except for reigning Olympic champion Neeraj Chopra, not many are top contenders in their respective disciplines. More than half of the 117-member contingent is from three sports — athletics (29), shooting (21) and hockey (19). Of these 69 athletes, 40 are debutants. ReadMore Here.













Indian archers Deepika Kumari, Ankita Bhakat and Bhajan Kaur are currently in action in the women’s individual ranking round.

This is the fourth Olympic each for Deepika Kumari and Tarundeep Rai. For Pravin Jadhav, it’s his second Olympics after Tokyo while Ankita Bhakat, Dhiraj Bommadevara and Bhajan Kaur are making their debuts.

It’s for the first time since the London Olympics in 2012 that India have six archers in action at the quadrennial sporting spectacle which means they will be challenging for a medal in all five events (women’s individual, women’s team, men’s individual, men’s team, mixed team).
